race_note: Wood Elf (non-Ryndor race, GM-approved). +2 DEX, +1 WIS. Darkvision 60 ft. Keen Senses (Perception prof). Fey Ancestry (adv. vs. charm, immune to magical sleep). Trance (4 hr rest). Fleet of Foot (speed 35 ft). Mask of the Wild (hide in light natural obscurement). Weapon training: longsword, shortsword, shortbow, longbow. Languages: Common, Elvish.
stat_notes: Pre-racial: STR 12, DEX 13, CON 15, INT 10, WIS 14, CHA 8. Wood Elf: +2 DEX, +1 WIS.
weapon_flavor: Sturdy hardwood flute, carved from Holmea ironwood — declared equivalent to a mace (simple melee, 1d6 bludgeoning). Attack: +3 (STR +1 + prof +2). The flute is played and swung as a single instrument; techniques that produce sound or wind emit from the bore of the flute itself.
inv_gear: crystal vial (small permanent storm inside — failed initiate ritual, never dispelled)

chosen_techniques.name: Gust Push
chosen_techniques.note: Bonus action, 20 ft range, STR save or pushed 5 ft. Opens fights and sets up positioning.

chosen_techniques.name: Sonic Clap
chosen_techniques.note: Bonus action, 5 ft radius, CON save or 1d6 thunder + deafened until start of your next turn. Thematic: Eurus plays a resonant shock chord on the flute. Use when surrounded.

chosen_techniques.name: Wind Dance
chosen_techniques.note: Bonus action: Dash + ignore difficult terrain + no OA all turn. Core mobility technique. Pair with a melee attack.

chosen_techniques.name: Lightning Lash
chosen_techniques.note: Bonus action, 15 ft, ranged technique attack. Hit: 2d6 lightning + target loses reactions until start of its next turn. Shuts down counterspell and shield.

chosen_techniques.name: Ride the Storm
chosen_techniques.note: Bonus action. For 1 minute, movement no longer provokes OA and you can move through occupied spaces. The signature L5 mobility upgrade — pair with melee pressure.

chosen_techniques.name: Thunderbolt Strike
chosen_techniques.note: Bonus action. Charge next weapon attack with lightning. On hit: extra lightning damage and target may be stunned/slowed. Direct upgrade to the flute attack loop.

elemental_charges.notes: 4 charges at level 5 (5th–6th row). 6 techniques known. All restored on short or long rest. Weirding Surge: roll d6 on every charge spend.

shift_preference: Lock — Tempest Strike (6) in melee for prone/push on every hit. Cyclone's Touch (1) when mobility is more valuable than damage.
notes: Eurus Polvo attended a Sev'rinn initiate house in Holmea for three years, during which she was written up eleven times for 'unsanctioned elemental discharge in communal areas,' graduated anyway because her air attunement was the strongest her instructor had seen in a decade, and was politely asked not to come back for the reunion. The flute was technically in the initiate house music room when she left; she considers this a detail of diminishing relevance. The crystal vial from her kit has had a small permanent storm inside it since her second year — a failed dispel ritual she gave up on and now considers a personality trait.
personality: I name gusts of wind. Not every gust — just the ones with character. 'That's Miravel,' I'll say, 'she comes in from the northeast like she's annoyed about something.' I also commentate weather out loud in real time. People find this unsettling. I find it useful.
ideals: Passage. Everything that moves — wind, sound, water, people — has a right to keep moving. I will always open a closed window. I have never once met a door I thought should stay shut.
bonds: The flute and I have an understanding: it screams at the worst possible moments and I carry it anyway. It has embarrassed me at two diplomatic meetings, one funeral, and what I was told was a very quiet monastery. I think it likes me.
flaws: I have no indoor voice. This is not a figure of speech — I have genuinely never located one. The attunement makes it worse: when I get excited the air pressure in the room changes noticeably. At least three tavern owners in Holmea have put something on their door with my face on it. I choose to take this as recognition.

combat_tactics.role: Mobile skirmisher and battlefield disruptor. Eurus uses terrain and positioning to stay dangerous at both melee and short range, peeling apart enemy formations with wind and lightning while the flute keeps swinging.
combat_tactics.charge_economy: 4 charges at L5. Entering Shift costs 1 → 3 remaining. L1 techniques cost 1, L3–5 cost varies. Typical budget: Shift + 3 technique uses. Refill on short rest.

tactics.text: Bonus action: Activate Windform (1C → 2 remaining). Lock on Tempest Strike (6) — on every melee hit, knock the target prone OR push them 10 ft back. Prone targets take attacks with advantage and stand up spending half their movement; pushed targets may fall off ledges or break formation. Move 35 ft to the highest-value melee target. Main action: Attack with flute (+3, 1d6+1 bludgeoning). On a hit, apply Tempest Strike.

Gust Push (1C, bonus action): Push a target 5 ft in any direction. Use to push into difficult terrain, into another enemy's space to cause squeeze, off a ledge for fall damage, or simply away from an ally. Can be used before activating Shift if you want to set up the field before engaging. Range is 20 ft — no need to be adjacent.

Wind Dance (1C, bonus action, 2/short rest): Dash with zero OA risk and ignore difficult terrain for the entire turn. Use to close on a target behind a battle line, escape being surrounded, or reposition across a broken battlefield. Combine with the main-action attack: Wind Dance in, swing, and you've moved up to 70 ft this turn (35 + 35 Dash) without provoking a single opportunity attack.

Lightning Lash (1C, bonus action, 2/short rest, 15 ft): Ranged spell attack at +4. On a hit: 2d6 lightning and the target loses its reaction until the start of its next turn. Use this against any enemy who relies on reactions — counterspell, shield, hellish rebuke, attacks of opportunity. A spellcaster who can't counterspell is suddenly much less threatening. Also good when Eurus can't safely reach melee range.

Sonic Clap (1C, bonus action): 5 ft radius AoE, CON save (DC 12) or 1d6 thunder + deafened. When two or more enemies are adjacent, lead with this before your main attack. Deafened creatures automatically fail STR and DEX checks that require hearing and have disadvantage on Perception checks — not a showstopper, but the thunder damage softens everyone. Then Wind Dance out if needed.

Every charge you spend requires a d6 roll, and the result on the Surge Table always occurs — no avoiding it. At 3 charges per encounter, you'll roll 3 times. The table is not random noise to survive; it's part of Eurus's combat texture. Learn what the results do and build around them. At level 3 with limited charges, playing scared is worse than leaning into a bad roll.

All charges restore on a short rest. If you have 0 charges and the party takes even a short rest, you're back to 3. Wind Dance and Lightning Lash restore 2 uses each on a short rest. Plan around this — push hard in each encounter, then rest. Don't hoard charges across multiple fights expecting them to stack.

Once per long rest, activate as a bonus action for 1 minute: all movement is OA-free and you pass through occupied spaces. Use in any fight where positioning is contested. Combine with Wind Dance or standard movement to thread through enemy lines, reach the backline, and swing the flute. DC is now 14 (CON 17, prof +3).

Bonus action before your weapon attack: next hit deals extra lightning damage. Works with the standard flute attack loop — use when Lightning Lash isn't available and you're already in melee. 2/short rest. Attack is now +4 (STR +1, prof +3). Technique attack is +6 (CON +3, prof +3).
